behaviors.description: TSP heuristic produces route ≤1.5x optimal for ≤20 stops
behaviors.justification: Pure algorithm with no I/O or external dependencies. TSP quality can be verified against published benchmark instances (e.g., TSPLIB) using synthetic coordinates and known optimal route lengths. The test runs entirely in memory—no geocoding API, real addresses, or external infrastructure needed.
behaviors.test_description: Use standard TSP benchmark instances (up to 20 stops) with published optimal solutions. Provide coordinates to the TSP algorithm, compute the returned route length, and assert it does not exceed 1.5× the known optimal. Test multiple instances covering various problem structures (grids, circles, clusters).

behaviors.description: Time window estimation formula correct from distance and speed
behaviors.justification: Pure calculation function: time = distance / speed (plus any road-type adjustments). No external calls or I/O needed. The function receives distance and speed as parameters; it is not responsible for computing distance from coordinates.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrize over distance values (0, small, large), speed values (slow, average, fast), and road types if applicable. Call the time estimation function and verify the output matches the expected formula. Include edge cases (zero distance, speed overrides per road type).

behaviors.description: Shift validator rejects route exceeding remaining hours, returns excess
behaviors.justification: Pure validation logic: compare total route time against remaining shift hours, return reject/accept and excess amount. No I/O, external services, or multi-module coordination needed.
behaviors.test_description: Create test cases: (route_time=5h, remaining=3h) should reject with excess=2h; (route_time=2h, remaining=5h) should accept; edge case (route_time=5h, remaining=5h) should accept. Call the validator and assert decisions and excess amounts.

behaviors.description: Failed geocoding: skip address, optimize remaining, report skip
behaviors.justification: The skip logic alone (Unit) could pass while downstream failures hide: the TSP might not receive the reduced address list, or the reporting step could fail. Real failures occur when the failed address is removed from the input, propagates through TSP correctly, and is reported. Must test the full pipeline: geocoding → skip → TSP → reporting.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the geocoding API to return 'no results' for one address in a multi-address input. Run the full optimization pipeline. Assert: (1) the failed address is not in the final route, (2) remaining addresses are present in an optimized route, (3) the skip report lists the correct address. This catches failures in address-list mutation, TSP integration, and reporting.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses consolidated to single stop, single visit
behaviors.justification: The consolidation logic (Unit) could work, but the consolidated result must propagate through TSP to produce a route with single visits. A Unit test of just the deduplication function would miss failures if TSP receives the original unconsolidated list, or if the route-building merges stops back. Must test the full pipeline: consolidation → TSP → route verification.
behaviors.test_description: Input a list with duplicates: [addr1+item1, addr1+item2, addr2+item3]. Run the full optimization pipeline. Assert: (1) the final route contains addr1 exactly once (not two separate stops), (2) delivery items for addr1 are combined, (3) TSP visits addr1 in a single pass. Test with multiple duplicates and various positions in the list.
